```text
     1	import { execFileSync, spawn } from 'node:child_process';
     2	import { backendEnv, DATABASE_URL, resolveE2EPaths } from './stack.shared.mjs';
     3	
     4	const { backendDir, composeFile, composeProject } = resolveE2EPaths();
     5	
     6	function run(command, args, env = process.env) {
     7	  execFileSync(command, args, {
     8	    cwd: backendDir,
     9	    env,
    10	    stdio: 'inherit',
    11	  });
    12	}
    13	
    14	function teardownInfra() {
    15	  run('docker', ['compose', '-f', composeFile, '-p', composeProject, 'down', '-v']);
    16	}
    17	
    18	run('docker', ['compose', '-f', composeFile, '-p', composeProject, 'up', '-d', '--wait']);
    19	run('npx', ['prisma', 'migrate', 'reset', '--force', '--skip-generate', '--skip-seed'], {
    20	  ...process.env,
    21	  DATABASE_URL,
    22	});
    23	run('npm', ['run', 'db:seed:mock'], {
    24	  ...process.env,
    25	  DATABASE_URL,
    26	});
    27	
    28	const backend = spawn('npm', ['run', 'dev'], {
    29	  cwd: backendDir,
    30	  env: backendEnv(),
    31	  stdio: 'inherit',
    32	});
    33	
    34	for (const signal of ['SIGINT', 'SIGTERM']) {
    35	  process.on(signal, () => {
    36	    backend.kill(signal);
    37	  });
    38	}
    39	
    40	backend.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
    41	  teardownInfra();
    42	
    43	  if (signal) {
    44	    process.exit(0);
    45	    return;
    46	  }
    47	
    48	  process.exit(code ?? 0);
    49	});
```

```text
    25	**Rule / Fix:** For stateful E2E stacks, keep shared ports/URLs/test credentials in one cross-runtime JS module and force the backend launcher to restart/reset locally unless there is an explicit reason to preserve state.
```

#### 🤖 gpt-5.4 (high)

- Time: 2026-04-01T00:25:37.945Z

Addressed the review’s main gaps.

The E2E stack constants and credentials are now centralized in [stack.shared.mjs](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/harmony-frontend/tests/e2e/support/stack.shared.mjs#L1), and both the Playwright config and backend launcher consume that single source of truth. The backend launcher in [start-backend-e2e.mjs](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/harmony-frontend/tests/e2e/support/start-backend-e2e.mjs#L1) still brings up isolated Postgres/Redis, resets Prisma, seeds mock data, starts the real backend, and now tears infra down in backend-first order. In [playwright.config.ts](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/harmony-frontend/playwright.config.ts#L1) I changed the backend web server to `reuseExistingServer: false`, so local reruns reseed cleanly instead of leaking signup state, and I expanded the browser matrix to `chromium` plus `webkit`.

I also removed the ad hoc credentials from the test body by importing named values from the shared module, and made the signup identity project-specific in [auth.spec.ts](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/harmony-frontend/tests/e2e/auth.spec.ts#L1), so cross-browser runs no longer collide on the same registered email. I logged the lesson in [tasks/lessons.md](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/tasks/lessons.md#L21).

Verified:
- `harmony-frontend: npm run lint`
- `harmony-frontend: npm run build`
- `harmony-frontend: npm run test:e2e`
- `harmony-frontend: npm run test:e2e` again locally to confirm rerun safety

Current result: 8 E2E tests passing across Chromium and WebKit. Remaining noise is non-fatal: the existing `getMessages` fallback log and a `MaxListenersExceededWarning` from the dev tooling during Playwright startup.

### Turn 5: The agent also read additional files on disk (seed data, shared

#### 👤 User Prompt

- Time: 2026-04-01T03:45:53.586Z

```text
The agent also read additional files on disk (seed data, shared constants). Here's the consolidated review:

  ---
  Critical

  Admin credentials don't exist in the seed
  auth.spec.ts:41-42 uses admin@harmony.dev / admin, but the seed data has no such user — the admin account is seeded under a different email/password. This test
  will always fail against a clean seed.

  /c/ vs /channels/ URL inconsistency
  Tests 1 and 2 navigate to /c/harmony-hq/..., test 3 navigates to /channels/harmony-hq/staff-only. The returnUrl assertions encode whichever prefix was used, so at
  least one set of URL assertions will always be wrong unless both are valid aliases.

  ---
  High

  Signup test fails on CI retry
  retries: 2 on CI. If the signup test fails for any reason and retries, the second attempt hits a unique constraint on e2e-member@harmony.test — the first attempt
  already wrote the row. The test can never pass on retry.

  teardownInfra skipped when setup throws
  If any of the execFileSync calls in start-backend-e2e.mjs (docker compose up, prisma reset, seed) throws, the process exits without calling teardownInfra(). Docker
   containers are left running. There's no process.on('uncaughtException') or process.on('exit') guard.

  ---
  Medium

  Seed strings are magic values in tests
  'harmony-hq', 'general', 'staff-only', 'Welcome to Harmony HQ!' are inline in the spec but live in the seed data JSON. A seed rename silently breaks tests.

  No globalSetup to verify preconditions
  If the seed exits 0 but didn't actually apply (e.g., silent failure), all tests fail with confusing navigation errors instead of "seed did not run."

  Coverage gaps still present: no logout test, no session persistence (reload while logged in), no message-send flow, no duplicate-email signup path.

  ---
  Low

  NODE_ENV: 'development' in E2E backend — should be 'e2e' or 'test' to avoid activating dev-only code paths.

  No Page Object Model — signUpMember is a lone helper; as the suite grows, POM structure will be needed to avoid duplication.

  No storageState reuse — tests that need auth as a precondition (not testing login itself) should cache a session rather than driving the full login UI each time.

  ---
  Summary Table

  ┌──────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ Priority │                          Issue                          │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Critical │ Admin credentials absent from seed                      │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Critical │ /c/ vs /channels/ URL mismatch                          │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ High     │ Signup test breaks on CI retry                          │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ High     │ teardownInfra not called on setup exception             │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Medium   │ Seed slugs/text hardcoded as magic strings              │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Medium   │ No globalSetup precondition check                       │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Medium   │ Missing logout, session persistence, message-send tests │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Low      │ NODE_ENV should be 'e2e'                                │
  ├──────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Low      │ No POM, no storageState reuse                           │
  └──────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Another review left by claude
```

#### 🤖 gpt-5.4 (high)

- Time: 2026-04-01T03:51:01.038Z

Most of that review is now addressed.

The valid issues were:
- CI retry safety: signup identities now include project and retry suffixes in [auth.spec.ts](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/harmony-frontend/tests/e2e/auth.spec.ts#L4), so retries no longer collide.
- Setup/teardown robustness: the backend launcher now has explicit preflight checks plus fatal-error teardown handling in [start-backend-e2e.mjs](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/harmony-frontend/tests/e2e/support/start-backend-e2e.mjs#L1).
- Magic seed strings: the suite now reads shared fixture values from [stack.shared.mjs](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/harmony-frontend/tests/e2e/support/stack.shared.mjs#L1) instead of scattering them through the spec.
- Local rerun safety: backend reuse is disabled in [playwright.config.ts](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/harmony-frontend/playwright.config.ts#L30), so every local run reseeds.
- Browser coverage: the matrix now includes WebKit as well as Chromium in [playwright.config.ts](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/harmony-frontend/playwright.config.ts#L16).

Two “critical” findings were not actually defects:
- `admin@harmony.dev / admin` is a dev-only login override in [auth.service.ts](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/harmony-backend/src/services/auth.service.ts#L169), not a seeded account.
- `/c/...` vs `/channels/...` is intentional route separation: guest view lives in [c/[serverSlug]/[channelSlug]/page.tsx](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/harmony-frontend/src/app/c/[serverSlug]/[channelSlug]/page.tsx#L39) and authenticated view in [channels/[serverSlug]/[channelSlug]/page.tsx](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/harmony-frontend/src/app/channels/[serverSlug]/[channelSlug]/page.tsx#L7).

Current verification:
- `npm run lint`
- `npm run build`
- `npm run test:e2e`

Result: 8 passing E2E tests across Chromium and WebKit. I also recorded the rerun/duplication lesson in [lessons.md](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/tasks/lessons.md#L21).

Remaining non-blocking gaps are still coverage-related: logout, session persistence, message send, and duplicate-email UX.

### Turn 6: Good improvements since last time. Here's the updated assessment:

#### 👤 User Prompt

- Time: 2026-04-01T03:53:45.431Z

```text
Good improvements since last time. Here's the updated assessment:

  Resolved Since Last Review

  - Constants duplication — stack.shared.mjs is now the single source of truth for all ports, URLs, credentials, and seed fixtures
  - Magic strings — all slugs/names/text are derived from the seed JSON at import time, with immediate throws if anything is missing
  - Signup retry failure — signUpMember now namespaces by project.name + retry count, so each attempt gets a unique email/username
  - teardownInfra on setup failure — handleFatal + uncaughtException/unhandledRejection handlers + setupFinished guard cover the gap
  - Seed precondition check — verifySeedPreconditions() polls the live backend to confirm server, public channel, and private channel (403) all exist before tests
  run
  - WebKit added — both Chromium and Safari/WebKit now covered

  ---
  Remaining Issues

  Admin credentials still unverified in preconditions (medium)
  verifySeedPreconditions confirms the server and channels exist, but never verifies that admin@harmony.dev / admin is a valid login. If the seed doesn't include
  that user, test 3 passes preflight and then fails at login with a confusing auth error rather than a clear "admin not seeded" message.

  backendEnv() defaults to NODE_ENV: 'development' (low)
  stack.shared.mjs:39 sets NODE_ENV: 'development' inside backendEnv(). start-backend-e2e.mjs overrides it with 'e2e' — correct, but it's a subtle trap. Anyone else
  calling backendEnv() would get the wrong NODE_ENV for E2E runs. Better to remove it from backendEnv() and let the caller set it explicitly.

  loadSeedSnapshot() runs at import time and path is cwd-relative (low)
  stack.shared.mjs:71 — const snapshot = loadSeedSnapshot() executes when the module is first imported. If mock-seed-data.json doesn't exist (fresh clone, pre-seed),
   playwright.config.ts fails to load entirely with a raw ENOENT instead of a helpful message. A try/catch with a clear "run db:seed:mock first" error would make
  this much friendlier.

  Plus-addressing uniqueness depends on backend behavior (low)
  signUpMember generates e2e-member+chromium-r0@harmony.test. If the backend normalises plus addresses (strips the +tag), chromium and webkit runs collide on the
  same canonical email. This is safe only if the backend treats the full string as the unique identifier.

  Coverage gaps remain
  - No logout test
  - No session persistence (reload while authenticated)
  - No message-send flow
  - No duplicate-email signup rejection

  ---
  Summary

  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────┐
  │                   Issue                    │       Status        │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ Constants duplication                      │ Fixed               │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ Magic strings                              │ Fixed               │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ Signup CI retry failure                    │ Fixed               │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ teardownInfra on setup throw               │ Fixed               │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ Seed preconditions                         │ Fixed               │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ WebKit coverage                            │ Fixed               │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ Admin credentials unverified               │ Still open (medium) │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ backendEnv NODE_ENV trap                   │ Still open (low)    │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ loadSeedSnapshot bad error on missing file │ Still open (low)    │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ Plus-address uniqueness assumption         │ Still open (low)    │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┤
  │ Logout / session / message-send tests      │ Still open          │
  └────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────┘

  The suite is in solid shape. The most actionable remaining item is verifying the admin login in verifySeedPreconditions.

More reviews
```

Output:

```text
import fs from 'node:fs';
import path from 'node:path';

export const FRONTEND_PORT = 3100;
export const BACKEND_PORT = 4100;
export const POSTGRES_PORT = 55432;
export const REDIS_PORT = 56379;

export const FRONTEND_URL = `http://localhost:${FRONTEND_PORT}`;
export const BACKEND_URL = `http://localhost:${BACKEND_PORT}`;
export const DATABASE_URL = `postgresql://harmony:harmony@localhost:${POSTGRES_PORT}/harmony_e2e`;
export const REDIS_URL = `redis://:e2esecret@localhost:${REDIS_PORT}`;

export const DEV_ADMIN_EMAIL = 'admin@harmony.dev';
export const DEV_ADMIN_PASSWORD = 'admin';

export const SIGNUP_USER = {
  email: 'e2e-member@harmony.test',
  username: 'e2e_member',
  displayName: 'E2E Member',
  password: 'password123',
};

export function cleanEnv(env = process.env) {
  return Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(env).filter(([, value]) => typeof value === 'string'));
}

export function frontendEnv(env = process.env) {
  return {
    ...cleanEnv(env),
    PORT: String(FRONTEND_PORT),
    NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL: BACKEND_URL,
  };
}

export function backendEnv(env = process.env) {
  return {
    ...cleanEnv(env),
    NODE_ENV: 'development',
    PORT: String(BACKEND_PORT),
    DATABASE_URL,
    REDIS_URL,
    FRONTEND_URL,
    JWT_ACCESS_SECRET: 'harmony-e2e-access-secret',
    JWT_REFRESH_SECRET: 'harmony-e2e-refresh-secret',
    JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRES_IN: '15m',
    JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRES_DAYS: '7',
    TWILIO_MOCK: 'true',
    LOCAL_UPLOAD_BASE_URL: BACKEND_URL,
  };
}

export function resolveE2EPaths(cwd = process.cwd()) {
  const repoRoot = path.resolve(cwd, '..');
  const backendDir = path.join(repoRoot, 'harmony-backend');

  return {
    repoRoot,
    backendDir,
    composeFile: path.join(backendDir, 'docker-compose.e2e.yml'),
    composeProject: 'harmony-e2e',
  };
}

function loadSeedSnapshot() {
  const { backendDir } = resolveE2EPaths();
  const seedPath = path.join(backendDir, 'src/dev/mock-seed-data.json');
  return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(seedPath, 'utf8'));
}

const snapshot = loadSeedSnapshot();

// Registration auto-joins this server in backend auth.service; keep this value
// aligned with that backend contract and fail fast in launcher preflight if it drifts.
export const DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG = 'harmony-hq';

const defaultJoinServer = snapshot.servers.find(server => server.slug === DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG);
if (!defaultJoinServer) {
  throw new Error(
    `Seed snapshot is missing the default join server slug "${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}".`,
  );
}

const publicChannel = snapshot.channels.find(
  channel => channel.serverId === defaultJoinServer.id && channel.visibility === 'PUBLIC_INDEXABLE',
);
if (!publicChannel) {
  throw new Error(
    `Seed snapshot is missing a public channel for server "${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}".`,
  );
}

const privateChannel = snapshot.channels.find(
  channel => channel.serverId === defaultJoinServer.id && channel.visibility === 'PRIVATE',
);
if (!privateChannel) {
  throw new Error(
    `Seed snapshot is missing a private channel for server "${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}".`,
  );
}

const welcomeMessage = snapshot.messages.find(message => message.channelId === publicChannel.id);
if (!welcomeMessage) {
  throw new Error(`Seed snapshot is missing a message for public channel "${publicChannel.slug}".`);
}

export const SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL = {
  serverSlug: defaultJoinServer.slug,
  serverName: defaultJoinServer.name,
  channelSlug: publicChannel.slug,
  channelName: publicChannel.name,
  welcomeText: welcomeMessage.content,
};

export const SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL = {
  serverSlug: defaultJoinServer.slug,
  channelName: privateChannel.name,
  channelSlug: privateChannel.slug,
};
```

#### Tool: exec_command

```sh
sed -n '1,260p' harmony-frontend/tests/e2e/support/start-backend-e2e.mjs
```

Output:

```text
import { execFileSync, spawn } from 'node:child_process';
import {
  backendEnv,
  BACKEND_URL,
  DATABASE_URL,
  DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG,
  resolveE2EPaths,
  SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL,
  SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL,
} from './stack.shared.mjs';

const { backendDir, composeFile, composeProject } = resolveE2EPaths();
let setupFinished = false;
let teardownFinished = false;
let backend;
let fatalExitCode = 0;

function run(command, args, env = process.env) {
  execFileSync(command, args, {
    cwd: backendDir,
    env,
    stdio: 'inherit',
  });
}

function teardownInfra() {
  if (teardownFinished || !setupFinished) return;
  teardownFinished = true;
  run('docker', ['compose', '-f', composeFile, '-p', composeProject, 'down', '-v']);
}

async function waitFor(url, predicate, label, timeoutMs = 30_000) {
  const startedAt = Date.now();

  while (Date.now() - startedAt < timeoutMs) {
    try {
      const response = await fetch(url);
      if (await predicate(response)) return;
    } catch {}

    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 500));
  }

  throw new Error(`Timed out waiting for ${label}.`);
}

async function verifySeedPreconditions() {
  await waitFor(`${BACKEND_URL}/health`, async response => response.ok, 'backend health check');

  await waitFor(
    `${BACKEND_URL}/api/public/servers/${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}`,
    async response => {
      if (!response.ok) return false;
      const body = await response.json();
      return body.slug === DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG;
    },
    'default join server fixture',
  );

  await waitFor(
    `${BACKEND_URL}/api/public/servers/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/channels/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`,
    async response => {
      if (!response.ok) return false;
      const body = await response.json();
      return body.slug === SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug;
    },
    'public channel fixture',
  );

  await waitFor(
    `${BACKEND_URL}/api/public/servers/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/channels/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`,
    async response => response.status === 403,
    'private channel fixture',
  );
}

function handleFatal(error) {
  console.error(error);
  fatalExitCode = 1;
  if (backend) {
    backend.kill('SIGTERM');
    return;
  }

  teardownInfra();
  process.exit(fatalExitCode);
}

async function main() {
  run('docker', ['compose', '-f', composeFile, '-p', composeProject, 'up', '-d', '--wait']);
  setupFinished = true;

  run('npx', ['prisma', 'migrate', 'reset', '--force', '--skip-generate', '--skip-seed'], {
    ...process.env,
    DATABASE_URL,
  });
  run('npm', ['run', 'db:seed:mock'], {
    ...process.env,
    DATABASE_URL,
  });

  backend = spawn('npm', ['run', 'dev'], {
    cwd: backendDir,
    env: {
      ...backendEnv(),
      NODE_ENV: 'e2e',
    },
    stdio: 'inherit',
  });

  for (const signal of ['SIGINT', 'SIGTERM']) {
    process.on(signal, () => {
      backend.kill(signal);
    });
  }

  process.on('uncaughtException', handleFatal);
  process.on('unhandledRejection', handleFatal);

  await verifySeedPreconditions();

  backend.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
    teardownInfra();

    if (fatalExitCode !== 0) {
      process.exit(fatalExitCode);
      return;
    }

    if (signal) {
      process.exit(0);
      return;
    }

    process.exit(code ?? 0);
  });
}

void main().catch(handleFatal);
```

#### Tool: exec_command

```sh
sed -n '1,180p' harmony-frontend/tests/e2e/auth.spec.ts
```

Output:

```text
import { expect, test, type Page, type TestInfo } from '@playwright/test';
import {
  DEV_ADMIN_EMAIL,
  DEV_ADMIN_PASSWORD,
  DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG,
  SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL,
  SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL,
  SIGNUP_USER,
} from './support/stack.shared.mjs';

async function signUpMember(page: Page, testInfo: TestInfo) {
  const { project, retry } = testInfo;
  const normalizedProject = project.name.replace(/[^a-z0-9_-]/gi, '-').toLowerCase();
  const normalizedRetry = `r${retry}`;
  const email = SIGNUP_USER.email.replace('@', `+${normalizedProject}-${normalizedRetry}@`);
  const username = `${SIGNUP_USER.username}_${normalizedProject}_${normalizedRetry}`.slice(0, 32);

  await page.goto('/auth/signup');
  await page.getByLabel(/^Email/).fill(email);
  await page.getByLabel(/^Username/).fill(username);
  await page.getByLabel('Display Name').fill(SIGNUP_USER.displayName);
  await page.getByLabel(/^Password/).fill(SIGNUP_USER.password);
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Continue' }).click();
}

test.describe('True E2E auth and access flows', () => {
  test('guest can read a seeded public channel', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto(`/c/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`);

    await expect(
      page.getByRole('banner').getByText(SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.serverName, { exact: true }),
    ).toBeVisible();
    await expect(
      page.getByRole('heading', { name: SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelName, exact: true }),
    ).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByText(SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.welcomeText).first()).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByLabel('Join server promotion')).toBeVisible();
  });

  test('guest is denied access to a private channel', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto(
      `/c/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`,
    );

    await expect(page.getByText('This channel is private')).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByRole('link', { name: 'Create Account' })).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByRole('link', { name: 'Log In' })).toHaveAttribute(
      'href',
      new RegExp(
        `returnUrl=%2Fc%2F${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}%2F${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}$`,
      ),
    );
  });

  test('dev admin can log in through the real backend and access a private channel', async ({
    page,
  }) => {
    await page.goto(
      `/channels/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`,
    );

    await expect(page).toHaveURL(
      new RegExp(
        `/auth/login\\?returnUrl=%2Fchannels%2F${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}%2F${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}$`,
      ),
    );

    await page.getByLabel('Email').fill(DEV_ADMIN_EMAIL);
    await page.getByLabel('Password').fill(DEV_ADMIN_PASSWORD);
    await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Log In' }).click();

    await expect(page).toHaveURL(
      new RegExp(
        `/channels/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}$`,
      ),
    );
    await expect(page.getByLabel(`Message #${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelName}`)).toBeVisible();
  });

  test('newly registered users join the seeded default server and land on the authenticated channel view', async ({
    page,
  }, testInfo) => {
    await signUpMember(page, testInfo);

    await expect(page).toHaveURL(
      new RegExp(`/channels/${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug}$`),
    );
    await expect(page.getByLabel(`Message #${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`)).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByText(SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.welcomeText).first()).toBeVisible();
  });
});
```

```text
## Log

<!-- Most recent entries at the top -->

**Date:** 2026-03-31  
**Caught by:** [AI Agent: Claude]  
**Related Issue:** N/A  
**Mistake / Situation:** The first true-E2E pass duplicated stack constants across TS and Node launcher files and allowed local server reuse, which made reruns stateful and brittle.  
**Rule / Fix:** For stateful E2E stacks, keep shared ports/URLs/test credentials in one cross-runtime JS module and force the backend launcher to restart/reset locally unless there is an explicit reason to preserve state.
```

#### 🤖 gpt-5.4 (high)

- Time: 2026-04-01T03:56:23.252Z

Closed the remaining actionable review items.

I added explicit admin-login preflight to the backend launcher in [start-backend-e2e.mjs](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/harmony-frontend/tests/e2e/support/start-backend-e2e.mjs#L1), so the suite now fails early with a clear auth-fixture error if `admin@harmony.dev / admin` stops working. I also removed the hidden `NODE_ENV` default from [stack.shared.mjs](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/harmony-frontend/tests/e2e/support/stack.shared.mjs#L1), wrapped seed snapshot loading with a clearer missing-file error, and changed signup identities in [auth.spec.ts](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/harmony-frontend/tests/e2e/auth.spec.ts#L1) to use distinct local parts instead of plus-addressing.

I also logged the new lesson in [lessons.md](/Users/allen/.codex/worktrees/373a/Harmony/tasks/lessons.md#L21).

Verified:
- `npm run lint`

```text
More reviews

Good — several previous issues are now fixed. Let me note what changed and what's left.

  Resolved Since Last Review

  ---
  Remaining Issues

  channelSlug vs channelName mismatch in message input assertions (medium)
  auth.spec.ts:81 uses SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelName for the aria-label lookup:
  getByLabel(`Message #${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelName}`)
  but auth.spec.ts:92 uses .channelSlug for the equivalent assertion on the public channel:
  getByLabel(`Message #${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`)
  If the channel name and slug differ (e.g. name is "General", slug is "general"), one of these will fail. They should both use whichever property the UI actually renders in the aria-label.

  /c/ vs /channels/ route asymmetry is still present (low/intentional?)
  Tests 1–2 navigate via /c/..., test 3 navigates via /channels/.... The returnUrl assertions encode whichever prefix was used, so the tests are internally consistent — but it's worth confirming this reflects two genuinely distinct routes (public read vs authenticated view) rather than an accidental
  inconsistency.

  SIGNUP_USER.email is defined but never used (low)
  stack.shared.mjs:18 defines email: 'e2e-member@harmony.test', but signUpMember now constructs its own email from SIGNUP_USER.username. The email field is dead. Either remove it or have signUpMember derive from it for clarity.

  Admin login preflight is one-shot, not retried (low)
  verifySeedPreconditions uses waitFor for the server/channel checks but does a single fetch for the admin login (line 78). If the auth module isn't fully wired up immediately after /health responds (uncommon but possible during NestJS cold start), this throws once rather than retrying. Wrapping it in waitFor
   would be consistent with the other checks.

  Coverage gaps remain
  No logout test, no session persistence (reload while authenticated), no message-send flow. These are the last meaningful holes in coverage for a chat app.
```

```text
/**
 * Channel Component: MessageInput
 * Message composition bar at the bottom of the channel view.
 * Supports multi-line input, Enter-to-send, character limit, and read-only guest state.
 * Ref: dev-spec-guest-public-channel-view.md — M3, CL-C3
 */

'use client';

import { useState, useRef, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react';
import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';
import { sendMessageAction } from '@/app/actions/sendMessage';
import type { Message } from '@/types';

const MAX_CHARS = 2000;
/** Show remaining-char indicator when this many characters remain */
const CHAR_WARN_THRESHOLD = 200;

export interface MessageInputProps {
  channelId: string;
  channelName: string;
  serverId: string;
  /** When true, replaces the input with a permission notice (guest / read-only views) */
  isReadOnly?: boolean;
  /** Called with the newly created message after a successful send */
  onMessageSent?: (message: Message) => void;
}

export function MessageInput({
  channelId,
  channelName,
  serverId,
  isReadOnly = false,
  onMessageSent,
}: MessageInputProps) {
  const [value, setValue] = useState('');
  const [isSending, setIsSending] = useState(false);
  const [sendError, setSendError] = useState<string | null>(null);
  const textareaRef = useRef<HTMLTextAreaElement>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    setValue('');
    setSendError(null);
    textareaRef.current?.focus();
  }, [channelId]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const el = textareaRef.current;
    if (!el) return;
    el.style.height = 'auto';
    el.style.height = `${Math.min(el.scrollHeight, 240)}px`;
  }, [value]);

  const handleSend = useCallback(async () => {
    const trimmed = value.trim();
    if (!trimmed || isSending || isReadOnly) return;
    setIsSending(true);
    setSendError(null);
    try {
      const msg = await sendMessageAction(channelId, trimmed, serverId);
      setValue('');
      onMessageSent?.(msg);
    } catch {
      setSendError('Failed to send message. Please try again.');
    } finally {
      setIsSending(false);
      textareaRef.current?.focus();
    }
  }, [value, isSending, isReadOnly, channelId, serverId, onMessageSent]);

  const handleKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent<HTMLTextAreaElement>) => {
    if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) {
      e.preventDefault();
      handleSend();
    }
  };

  const handleChange = (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLTextAreaElement>) => {
    if (e.target.value.length <= MAX_CHARS) {
      setValue(e.target.value);
    }
  };

  if (isReadOnly) {
    return (
      <div className='flex-shrink-0 px-4 pb-6 pt-2'>
        <div className='rounded-lg bg-[#40444b] px-4 py-3 text-center text-sm text-gray-400'>
          You do not have permission to send messages in this channel.
        </div>
      </div>
    );
  }

  const remaining = MAX_CHARS - value.length;
  const showCounter = remaining <= CHAR_WARN_THRESHOLD;
  const isAtLimit = remaining <= 0;

  return (
    <div className='flex-shrink-0 px-4 pb-6 pt-2'>
      {sendError && (
        <p className='mb-1 px-1 text-xs text-red-400' role='alert'>
          {sendError}
        </p>
      )}
      <div
        className={cn(
          'flex items-end gap-1 rounded-lg bg-[#40444b] px-2 py-2',
          isAtLimit && 'ring-1 ring-red-500/60',
        )}
      >
        <button
          type='button'
          title='Attach file (coming soon)'
          aria-label='Attach file'
          className='flex h-8 w-8 flex-shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-full text-gray-400 transition-colors hover:bg-white/10 hover:text-gray-200'
        >
          <svg className='h-5 w-5' viewBox='0 0 24 24' fill='currentColor'>
            <path d='M12 2C6.48 2 2 6.48 2 12s4.48 10 10 10 10-4.48 10-10S17.52 2 12 2zm5 11h-4v4h-2v-4H7v-2h4V7h2v4h4v2z' />
          </svg>
        </button>

        <textarea
          ref={textareaRef}
          value={value}
          onChange={handleChange}
          onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}
          placeholder={`Message #${channelName}`}
          rows={1}
          disabled={isSending}
          aria-label={`Message #${channelName}`}
          aria-multiline='true'
          className='flex-1 resize-none bg-transparent text-sm leading-relaxed text-[#dcddde] placeholder-gray-500 outline-none disabled:opacity-60'
          style={{ maxHeight: '240px', overflowY: 'auto' }}
        />

        <div className='flex flex-shrink-0 items-center gap-1'>
          {showCounter && (
            <span
              className={cn(
                'min-w-[2rem] text-right text-xs tabular-nums',
                isAtLimit ? 'text-red-400' : 'text-yellow-400',
              )}
              aria-live='polite'
              aria-label={`${remaining} characters remaining`}
            >
              {remaining}
            </span>
          )}

          <button
            type='button'
            title='Send GIF (coming soon)'
            aria-label='GIF'
            className='flex h-8 items-center justify-center rounded px-1.5 text-xs font-bold text-gray-400 transition-colors hover:bg-white/10 hover:text-gray-200'
          >
            GIF
          </button>

          <button
            type='button'
            title='Emoji (coming soon)'
            aria-label='Emoji'
            className='flex h-8 w-8 items-center justify-center rounded text-gray-400 transition-colors hover:bg-white/10 hover:text-gray-200'
          >
            <svg
              className='h-5 w-5'
              viewBox='0 0 24 24'
              fill='none'
              stroke='currentColor'
              strokeWidth={2}
              strokeLinecap='round'
              strokeLinejoin='round'
            >
              <circle cx='12' cy='12' r='10' />
              <path d='M8 13s1.5 2 4 2 4-2 4-2' />
              <line x1='9' y1='9' x2='9.01' y2='9' />
              <line x1='15' y1='9' x2='15.01' y2='9' />
            </svg>
          </button>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

Output:

```text
import fs from 'node:fs';
import path from 'node:path';

export const FRONTEND_PORT = 3100;
export const BACKEND_PORT = 4100;
export const POSTGRES_PORT = 55432;
export const REDIS_PORT = 56379;

export const FRONTEND_URL = `http://localhost:${FRONTEND_PORT}`;
export const BACKEND_URL = `http://localhost:${BACKEND_PORT}`;
export const DATABASE_URL = `postgresql://harmony:harmony@localhost:${POSTGRES_PORT}/harmony_e2e`;
export const REDIS_URL = `redis://:e2esecret@localhost:${REDIS_PORT}`;

export const DEV_ADMIN_EMAIL = 'admin@harmony.dev';
export const DEV_ADMIN_PASSWORD = 'admin';

export const SIGNUP_USER = {
  email: 'e2e-member@harmony.test',
  username: 'e2e_member',
  displayName: 'E2E Member',
  password: 'password123',
};

export function cleanEnv(env = process.env) {
  return Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(env).filter(([, value]) => typeof value === 'string'));
}

export function frontendEnv(env = process.env) {
  return {
    ...cleanEnv(env),
    PORT: String(FRONTEND_PORT),
    NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL: BACKEND_URL,
  };
}

export function backendEnv(env = process.env) {
  return {
    ...cleanEnv(env),
    PORT: String(BACKEND_PORT),
    DATABASE_URL,
    REDIS_URL,
    FRONTEND_URL,
    JWT_ACCESS_SECRET: 'harmony-e2e-access-secret',
    JWT_REFRESH_SECRET: 'harmony-e2e-refresh-secret',
    JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRES_IN: '15m',
    JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRES_DAYS: '7',
    TWILIO_MOCK: 'true',
    LOCAL_UPLOAD_BASE_URL: BACKEND_URL,
  };
}

export function resolveE2EPaths(cwd = process.cwd()) {
  const repoRoot = path.resolve(cwd, '..');
  const backendDir = path.join(repoRoot, 'harmony-backend');

  return {
    repoRoot,
    backendDir,
    composeFile: path.join(backendDir, 'docker-compose.e2e.yml'),
    composeProject: 'harmony-e2e',
  };
}

function loadSeedSnapshot() {
  const { backendDir } = resolveE2EPaths();
  const seedPath = path.join(backendDir, 'src/dev/mock-seed-data.json');
  try {
    return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(seedPath, 'utf8'));
  } catch (error) {
    if (error && typeof error === 'object' && 'code' in error && error.code === 'ENOENT') {
      throw new Error(
        `Missing E2E seed snapshot at ${seedPath}. Ensure the Harmony backend mock seed files are present before running Playwright.`,
      );
    }

    throw error;
  }
}

const snapshot = loadSeedSnapshot();

export const DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG = 'harmony-hq';

const defaultJoinServer = snapshot.servers.find(server => server.slug === DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG);
if (!defaultJoinServer) {
  throw new Error(
    `Seed snapshot is missing the default join server slug "${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}".`,
  );
}

const publicChannel = snapshot.channels.find(
  channel => channel.serverId === defaultJoinServer.id && channel.visibility === 'PUBLIC_INDEXABLE',
);
if (!publicChannel) {
  throw new Error(
    `Seed snapshot is missing a public channel for server "${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}".`,
  );
}

const privateChannel = snapshot.channels.find(
  channel => channel.serverId === defaultJoinServer.id && channel.visibility === 'PRIVATE',
);
if (!privateChannel) {
  throw new Error(
    `Seed snapshot is missing a private channel for server "${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}".`,
  );
}

const welcomeMessage = snapshot.messages.find(message => message.channelId === publicChannel.id);
if (!welcomeMessage) {
  throw new Error(`Seed snapshot is missing a message for public channel "${publicChannel.slug}".`);
}

export const SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL = {
  serverSlug: defaultJoinServer.slug,
  serverName: defaultJoinServer.name,
  channelSlug: publicChannel.slug,
  channelName: publicChannel.name,
  welcomeText: welcomeMessage.content,
};

export const SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL = {
  serverSlug: defaultJoinServer.slug,
  channelName: privateChannel.name,
  channelSlug: privateChannel.slug,
};
```

```text
import { expect, test, type Page, type TestInfo } from '@playwright/test';
import {
  DEV_ADMIN_EMAIL,
  DEV_ADMIN_PASSWORD,
  DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG,
  SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL,
  SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL,
  SIGNUP_USER,
} from './support/stack.shared.mjs';

async function signUpMember(page: Page, testInfo: TestInfo) {
  const { project, retry } = testInfo;
  const normalizedProject = project.name.replace(/[^a-z0-9_-]/gi, '-').toLowerCase();
  const normalizedRetry = `r${retry}`;
  const emailLocalPart = `${SIGNUP_USER.username}-${normalizedProject}-${normalizedRetry}`.replace(
    /_/g,
    '-',
  );
  const email = `${emailLocalPart}@harmony.test`;
  const username = `${SIGNUP_USER.username}_${normalizedProject}_${normalizedRetry}`.slice(0, 32);

  await page.goto('/auth/signup');
  await page.getByLabel(/^Email/).fill(email);
  await page.getByLabel(/^Username/).fill(username);
  await page.getByLabel('Display Name').fill(SIGNUP_USER.displayName);
  await page.getByLabel(/^Password/).fill(SIGNUP_USER.password);
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Continue' }).click();
}

test.describe('True E2E auth and access flows', () => {
  test('guest can read a seeded public channel', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto(`/c/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`);

    await expect(
      page.getByRole('banner').getByText(SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.serverName, { exact: true }),
    ).toBeVisible();
    await expect(
      page.getByRole('heading', { name: SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelName, exact: true }),
    ).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByText(SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.welcomeText).first()).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByLabel('Join server promotion')).toBeVisible();
  });

  test('guest is denied access to a private channel', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto(
      `/c/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`,
    );

    await expect(page.getByText('This channel is private')).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByRole('link', { name: 'Create Account' })).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByRole('link', { name: 'Log In' })).toHaveAttribute(
      'href',
      new RegExp(
        `returnUrl=%2Fc%2F${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}%2F${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}$`,
      ),
    );
  });

  test('dev admin can log in through the real backend and access a private channel', async ({
    page,
  }) => {
    await page.goto(
      `/channels/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`,
    );

    await expect(page).toHaveURL(
      new RegExp(
        `/auth/login\\?returnUrl=%2Fchannels%2F${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}%2F${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}$`,
      ),
    );

    await page.getByLabel('Email').fill(DEV_ADMIN_EMAIL);
    await page.getByLabel('Password').fill(DEV_ADMIN_PASSWORD);
    await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Log In' }).click();

    await expect(page).toHaveURL(
      new RegExp(
        `/channels/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}$`,
      ),
    );
    await expect(page.getByLabel(`Message #${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelName}`)).toBeVisible();
  });

  test('newly registered users join the seeded default server and land on the authenticated channel view', async ({
    page,
  }, testInfo) => {
    await signUpMember(page, testInfo);

    await expect(page).toHaveURL(
      new RegExp(`/channels/${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug}$`),
    );
    await expect(page.getByLabel(`Message #${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`)).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByText(SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.welcomeText).first()).toBeVisible();
  });
});
```

Output:

```text
import { execFileSync, spawn } from 'node:child_process';
import {
  backendEnv,
  BACKEND_URL,
  DEV_ADMIN_EMAIL,
  DEV_ADMIN_PASSWORD,
  DATABASE_URL,
  DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG,
  resolveE2EPaths,
  SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL,
  SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL,
} from './stack.shared.mjs';

const { backendDir, composeFile, composeProject } = resolveE2EPaths();
let setupFinished = false;
let teardownFinished = false;
let backend;
let fatalExitCode = 0;

function run(command, args, env = process.env) {
  execFileSync(command, args, {
    cwd: backendDir,
    env,
    stdio: 'inherit',
  });
}

function teardownInfra() {
  if (teardownFinished || !setupFinished) return;
  teardownFinished = true;
  run('docker', ['compose', '-f', composeFile, '-p', composeProject, 'down', '-v']);
}

async function waitFor(url, predicate, label, timeoutMs = 30_000) {
  const startedAt = Date.now();

  while (Date.now() - startedAt < timeoutMs) {
    try {
      const response = await fetch(url);
      if (await predicate(response)) return;
    } catch {}

    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 500));
  }

  throw new Error(`Timed out waiting for ${label}.`);
}

async function verifySeedPreconditions() {
  await waitFor(`${BACKEND_URL}/health`, async response => response.ok, 'backend health check');

  await waitFor(
    `${BACKEND_URL}/api/public/servers/${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}`,
    async response => {
      if (!response.ok) return false;
      const body = await response.json();
      return body.slug === DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG;
    },
    'default join server fixture',
  );

  await waitFor(
    `${BACKEND_URL}/api/public/servers/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/channels/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`,
    async response => {
      if (!response.ok) return false;
      const body = await response.json();
      return body.slug === SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug;
    },
    'public channel fixture',
  );

  await waitFor(
    `${BACKEND_URL}/api/public/servers/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/channels/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`,
    async response => response.status === 403,
    'private channel fixture',
  );

  const loginResponse = await fetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/api/auth/login`, {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      email: DEV_ADMIN_EMAIL,
      password: DEV_ADMIN_PASSWORD,
    }),
  });

  if (!loginResponse.ok) {
    throw new Error(
      `Dev admin login preflight failed with status ${loginResponse.status}. Check the auth override fixture.`,
    );
  }

  const authPayload = await loginResponse.json();
  if (typeof authPayload.accessToken !== 'string' || typeof authPayload.refreshToken !== 'string') {
    throw new Error('Dev admin login preflight returned an invalid auth payload.');
  }
}

function handleFatal(error) {
  console.error(error);
  fatalExitCode = 1;
  if (backend) {
    backend.kill('SIGTERM');
    return;
  }

  teardownInfra();
  process.exit(fatalExitCode);
}

async function main() {
  run('docker', ['compose', '-f', composeFile, '-p', composeProject, 'up', '-d', '--wait']);
  setupFinished = true;

  run('npx', ['prisma', 'migrate', 'reset', '--force', '--skip-generate', '--skip-seed'], {
    ...process.env,
    DATABASE_URL,
  });
  run('npm', ['run', 'db:seed:mock'], {
    ...process.env,
    DATABASE_URL,
  });

  backend = spawn('npm', ['run', 'dev'], {
    cwd: backendDir,
    env: {
      ...backendEnv(),
      NODE_ENV: 'e2e',
    },
    stdio: 'inherit',
  });

  for (const signal of ['SIGINT', 'SIGTERM']) {
    process.on(signal, () => {
      backend.kill(signal);
    });
  }

  process.on('uncaughtException', handleFatal);
  process.on('unhandledRejection', handleFatal);

  await verifySeedPreconditions();

  backend.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
    teardownInfra();

    if (fatalExitCode !== 0) {
      process.exit(fatalExitCode);
      return;
    }

    if (signal) {
      process.exit(0);
      return;
    }

    process.exit(code ?? 0);
  });
}

void main().catch(handleFatal);
```

```text
import { expect, test, type Page, type TestInfo } from '@playwright/test';
import {
  DEV_ADMIN_EMAIL,
  DEV_ADMIN_PASSWORD,
  DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG,
  SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL,
  SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL,
  SIGNUP_USER,
} from './support/stack.shared.mjs';

async function signUpMember(page: Page, testInfo: TestInfo) {
  const { project, retry } = testInfo;
  const normalizedProject = project.name.replace(/[^a-z0-9_-]/gi, '-').toLowerCase();
  const normalizedRetry = `r${retry}`;
  const emailLocalPart = `${SIGNUP_USER.username}-${normalizedProject}-${normalizedRetry}`.replace(
    /_/g,
    '-',
  );
  const email = `${emailLocalPart}@harmony.test`;
  const username = `${SIGNUP_USER.username}_${normalizedProject}_${normalizedRetry}`.slice(0, 32);

  await page.goto('/auth/signup');
  await page.getByLabel(/^Email/).fill(email);
  await page.getByLabel(/^Username/).fill(username);
  await page.getByLabel('Display Name').fill(SIGNUP_USER.displayName);
  await page.getByLabel(/^Password/).fill(SIGNUP_USER.password);
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Continue' }).click();
}

test.describe('True E2E auth and access flows', () => {
  test('guest can read a seeded public channel', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto(`/c/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`);

    await expect(
      page.getByRole('banner').getByText(SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.serverName, { exact: true }),
    ).toBeVisible();
    await expect(
      page.getByRole('heading', { name: SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelName, exact: true }),
    ).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByText(SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.welcomeText).first()).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByLabel('Join server promotion')).toBeVisible();
  });

  test('guest is denied access to a private channel', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto(
      `/c/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`,
    );

    await expect(page.getByText('This channel is private')).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByRole('link', { name: 'Create Account' })).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByRole('link', { name: 'Log In' })).toHaveAttribute(
      'href',
      new RegExp(
        `returnUrl=%2Fc%2F${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}%2F${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}$`,
      ),
    );
  });

  test('dev admin can log in through the real backend and access a private channel', async ({
    page,
  }) => {
    await page.goto(
      `/channels/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`,
    );

    await expect(page).toHaveURL(
      new RegExp(
        `/auth/login\\?returnUrl=%2Fchannels%2F${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}%2F${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}$`,
      ),
    );

    await page.getByLabel('Email').fill(DEV_ADMIN_EMAIL);
    await page.getByLabel('Password').fill(DEV_ADMIN_PASSWORD);
    await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Log In' }).click();

    await expect(page).toHaveURL(
      new RegExp(
        `/channels/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}$`,
      ),
    );
    await expect(page.getByLabel(`Message #${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelName}`)).toBeVisible();
  });

  test('newly registered users join the seeded default server and land on the authenticated channel view', async ({
    page,
  }, testInfo) => {
    await signUpMember(page, testInfo);

    await expect(page).toHaveURL(
      new RegExp(`/channels/${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug}$`),
    );
    await expect(page.getByLabel(`Message #${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`)).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByText(SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.welcomeText).first()).toBeVisible();
  });
});
```

```text
import fs from 'node:fs';
import path from 'node:path';

export const FRONTEND_PORT = 3100;
export const BACKEND_PORT = 4100;
export const POSTGRES_PORT = 55432;
export const REDIS_PORT = 56379;

export const FRONTEND_URL = `http://localhost:${FRONTEND_PORT}`;
export const BACKEND_URL = `http://localhost:${BACKEND_PORT}`;
export const DATABASE_URL = `postgresql://harmony:harmony@localhost:${POSTGRES_PORT}/harmony_e2e`;
export const REDIS_URL = `redis://:e2esecret@localhost:${REDIS_PORT}`;

export const DEV_ADMIN_EMAIL = 'admin@harmony.dev';
export const DEV_ADMIN_PASSWORD = 'admin';

export const SIGNUP_USER = {
  email: 'e2e-member@harmony.test',
  username: 'e2e_member',
  displayName: 'E2E Member',
  password: 'password123',
};

export function cleanEnv(env = process.env) {
  return Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(env).filter(([, value]) => typeof value === 'string'));
}

export function frontendEnv(env = process.env) {
  return {
    ...cleanEnv(env),
    PORT: String(FRONTEND_PORT),
    NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL: BACKEND_URL,
  };
}

export function backendEnv(env = process.env) {
  return {
    ...cleanEnv(env),
    PORT: String(BACKEND_PORT),
    DATABASE_URL,
    REDIS_URL,
    FRONTEND_URL,
    JWT_ACCESS_SECRET: 'harmony-e2e-access-secret',
    JWT_REFRESH_SECRET: 'harmony-e2e-refresh-secret',
    JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRES_IN: '15m',
    JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRES_DAYS: '7',
    TWILIO_MOCK: 'true',
    LOCAL_UPLOAD_BASE_URL: BACKEND_URL,
  };
}

export function resolveE2EPaths(cwd = process.cwd()) {
  const repoRoot = path.resolve(cwd, '..');
  const backendDir = path.join(repoRoot, 'harmony-backend');

  return {
    repoRoot,
    backendDir,
    composeFile: path.join(backendDir, 'docker-compose.e2e.yml'),
    composeProject: 'harmony-e2e',
  };
}

function loadSeedSnapshot() {
  const { backendDir } = resolveE2EPaths();
  const seedPath = path.join(backendDir, 'src/dev/mock-seed-data.json');
  try {
    return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(seedPath, 'utf8'));
  } catch (error) {
    if (error && typeof error === 'object' && 'code' in error && error.code === 'ENOENT') {
      throw new Error(
        `Missing E2E seed snapshot at ${seedPath}. Ensure the Harmony backend mock seed files are present before running Playwright.`,
      );
    }

    throw error;
  }
}

const snapshot = loadSeedSnapshot();

export const DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG = 'harmony-hq';

const defaultJoinServer = snapshot.servers.find(server => server.slug === DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG);
if (!defaultJoinServer) {
  throw new Error(
    `Seed snapshot is missing the default join server slug "${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}".`,
  );
}

const publicChannel = snapshot.channels.find(
  channel => channel.serverId === defaultJoinServer.id && channel.visibility === 'PUBLIC_INDEXABLE',
);
if (!publicChannel) {
  throw new Error(
    `Seed snapshot is missing a public channel for server "${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}".`,
  );
}

const privateChannel = snapshot.channels.find(
  channel => channel.serverId === defaultJoinServer.id && channel.visibility === 'PRIVATE',
);
if (!privateChannel) {
  throw new Error(
    `Seed snapshot is missing a private channel for server "${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}".`,
  );
}

const welcomeMessage = snapshot.messages.find(message => message.channelId === publicChannel.id);
if (!welcomeMessage) {
  throw new Error(`Seed snapshot is missing a message for public channel "${publicChannel.slug}".`);
}

export const SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL = {
  serverSlug: defaultJoinServer.slug,
  serverName: defaultJoinServer.name,
  channelSlug: publicChannel.slug,
  channelName: publicChannel.name,
  welcomeText: welcomeMessage.content,
};

export const SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL = {
  serverSlug: defaultJoinServer.slug,
  channelName: privateChannel.name,
  channelSlug: privateChannel.slug,
};
```

#### Tool: exec_command

```sh
rtk read harmony-frontend/tests/e2e/support/start-backend-e2e.mjs
```

Output:

```text
import { execFileSync, spawn } from 'node:child_process';
import {
  backendEnv,
  BACKEND_URL,
  DEV_ADMIN_EMAIL,
  DEV_ADMIN_PASSWORD,
  DATABASE_URL,
  DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG,
  resolveE2EPaths,
  SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL,
  SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL,
} from './stack.shared.mjs';

const { backendDir, composeFile, composeProject } = resolveE2EPaths();
let setupFinished = false;
let teardownFinished = false;
let backend;
let fatalExitCode = 0;

function run(command, args, env = process.env) {
  execFileSync(command, args, {
    cwd: backendDir,
    env,
    stdio: 'inherit',
  });
}

function teardownInfra() {
  if (teardownFinished || !setupFinished) return;
  teardownFinished = true;
  run('docker', ['compose', '-f', composeFile, '-p', composeProject, 'down', '-v']);
}

async function waitFor(url, predicate, label, timeoutMs = 30_000) {
  const startedAt = Date.now();

  while (Date.now() - startedAt < timeoutMs) {
    try {
      const response = await fetch(url);
      if (await predicate(response)) return;
    } catch {}

    await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 500));
  }

  throw new Error(`Timed out waiting for ${label}.`);
}

async function verifySeedPreconditions() {
  await waitFor(`${BACKEND_URL}/health`, async response => response.ok, 'backend health check');

  await waitFor(
    `${BACKEND_URL}/api/public/servers/${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}`,
    async response => {
      if (!response.ok) return false;
      const body = await response.json();
      return body.slug === DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG;
    },
    'default join server fixture',
  );

  await waitFor(
    `${BACKEND_URL}/api/public/servers/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/channels/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`,
    async response => {
      if (!response.ok) return false;
      const body = await response.json();
      return body.slug === SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug;
    },
    'public channel fixture',
  );

  await waitFor(
    `${BACKEND_URL}/api/public/servers/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/channels/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`,
    async response => response.status === 403,
    'private channel fixture',
  );

  const loginResponse = await fetch(`${BACKEND_URL}/api/auth/login`, {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      email: DEV_ADMIN_EMAIL,
      password: DEV_ADMIN_PASSWORD,
    }),
  });

  if (!loginResponse.ok) {
    throw new Error(
      `Dev admin login preflight failed with status ${loginResponse.status}. Check the auth override fixture.`,
    );
  }

  const authPayload = await loginResponse.json();
  if (typeof authPayload.accessToken !== 'string' || typeof authPayload.refreshToken !== 'string') {
    throw new Error('Dev admin login preflight returned an invalid auth payload.');
  }
}

function handleFatal(error) {
  console.error(error);
  fatalExitCode = 1;
  if (backend) {
    backend.kill('SIGTERM');
    return;
  }

  teardownInfra();
  process.exit(fatalExitCode);
}

async function main() {
  run('docker', ['compose', '-f', composeFile, '-p', composeProject, 'up', '-d', '--wait']);
  setupFinished = true;

  run('npx', ['prisma', 'migrate', 'reset', '--force', '--skip-generate', '--skip-seed'], {
    ...process.env,
    DATABASE_URL,
  });
  run('npm', ['run', 'db:seed:mock'], {
    ...process.env,
    DATABASE_URL,
  });

  backend = spawn('npm', ['run', 'dev'], {
    cwd: backendDir,
    env: {
      ...backendEnv(),
      NODE_ENV: 'e2e',
    },
    stdio: 'inherit',
  });

  for (const signal of ['SIGINT', 'SIGTERM']) {
    process.on(signal, () => {
      backend.kill(signal);
    });
  }

  process.on('uncaughtException', handleFatal);
  process.on('unhandledRejection', handleFatal);

  await verifySeedPreconditions();

  backend.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
    teardownInfra();

    if (fatalExitCode !== 0) {
      process.exit(fatalExitCode);
      return;
    }

    if (signal) {
      process.exit(0);
      return;
    }

    process.exit(code ?? 0);
  });
}

void main().catch(handleFatal);
```

Output:

```text
/**
 * Channel Component: MessageInput
 * Message composition bar at the bottom of the channel view.
 * Supports multi-line input, Enter-to-send, character limit, and read-only guest state.
 * Ref: dev-spec-guest-public-channel-view.md — M3, CL-C3
 */

'use client';

import { useState, useRef, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react';
import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';
import { sendMessageAction } from '@/app/actions/sendMessage';
import type { Message } from '@/types';

const MAX_CHARS = 2000;
/** Show remaining-char indicator when this many characters remain */
const CHAR_WARN_THRESHOLD = 200;

export interface MessageInputProps {
  channelId: string;
  channelName: string;
  serverId: string;
  /** When true, replaces the input with a permission notice (guest / read-only views) */
  isReadOnly?: boolean;
  /** Called with the newly created message after a successful send */
  onMessageSent?: (message: Message) => void;
}

export function MessageInput({
  channelId,
  channelName,
  serverId,
  isReadOnly = false,
  onMessageSent,
}: MessageInputProps) {
  const [value, setValue] = useState('');
  const [isSending, setIsSending] = useState(false);
  const [sendError, setSendError] = useState<string | null>(null);
  const textareaRef = useRef<HTMLTextAreaElement>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    setValue('');
    setSendError(null);
    textareaRef.current?.focus();
  }, [channelId]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const el = textareaRef.current;
    if (!el) return;
    el.style.height = 'auto';
    el.style.height = `${Math.min(el.scrollHeight, 240)}px`;
  }, [value]);

  const handleSend = useCallback(async () => {
    const trimmed = value.trim();
    if (!trimmed || isSending || isReadOnly) return;
    setIsSending(true);
    setSendError(null);
    try {
      const msg = await sendMessageAction(channelId, trimmed, serverId);
      setValue('');
      onMessageSent?.(msg);
    } catch {
      setSendError('Failed to send message. Please try again.');
    } finally {
      setIsSending(false);
      textareaRef.current?.focus();
    }
  }, [value, isSending, isReadOnly, channelId, serverId, onMessageSent]);

  const handleKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent<HTMLTextAreaElement>) => {
    if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) {
      e.preventDefault();
      handleSend();
    }
  };

  const handleChange = (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLTextAreaElement>) => {
    if (e.target.value.length <= MAX_CHARS) {
      setValue(e.target.value);
    }
  };

  if (isReadOnly) {
    return (
      <div className='flex-shrink-0 px-4 pb-6 pt-2'>
        <div className='rounded-lg bg-[#40444b] px-4 py-3 text-center text-sm text-gray-400'>
          You do not have permission to send messages in this channel.
        </div>
      </div>
    );
  }

  const remaining = MAX_CHARS - value.length;
  const showCounter = remaining <= CHAR_WARN_THRESHOLD;
  const isAtLimit = remaining <= 0;

  return (
    <div className='flex-shrink-0 px-4 pb-6 pt-2'>
      {sendError && (
        <p className='mb-1 px-1 text-xs text-red-400' role='alert'>
          {sendError}
        </p>
      )}
      <div
        className={cn(
          'flex items-end gap-1 rounded-lg bg-[#40444b] px-2 py-2',
          isAtLimit && 'ring-1 ring-red-500/60',
        )}
      >
        <button
          type='button'
          title='Attach file (coming soon)'
          aria-label='Attach file'
          className='flex h-8 w-8 flex-shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-full text-gray-400 transition-colors hover:bg-white/10 hover:text-gray-200'
        >
          <svg className='h-5 w-5' viewBox='0 0 24 24' fill='currentColor'>
            <path d='M12 2C6.48 2 2 6.48 2 12s4.48 10 10 10 10-4.48 10-10S17.52 2 12 2zm5 11h-4v4h-2v-4H7v-2h4V7h2v4h4v2z' />
          </svg>
        </button>

        <textarea
          ref={textareaRef}
          value={value}
          onChange={handleChange}
          onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}
          placeholder={`Message #${channelName}`}
          rows={1}
          disabled={isSending}
          aria-label={`Message #${channelName}`}
          aria-multiline='true'
          className='flex-1 resize-none bg-transparent text-sm leading-relaxed text-[#dcddde] placeholder-gray-500 outline-none disabled:opacity-60'
          style={{ maxHeight: '240px', overflowY: 'auto' }}
        />

        <div className='flex flex-shrink-0 items-center gap-1'>
          {showCounter && (
            <span
              className={cn(
                'min-w-[2rem] text-right text-xs tabular-nums',
                isAtLimit ? 'text-red-400' : 'text-yellow-400',
              )}
              aria-live='polite'
              aria-label={`${remaining} characters remaining`}
            >
              {remaining}
            </span>
          )}

          <button
            type='button'
            title='Send GIF (coming soon)'
            aria-label='GIF'
            className='flex h-8 items-center justify-center rounded px-1.5 text-xs font-bold text-gray-400 transition-colors hover:bg-white/10 hover:text-gray-200'
          >
            GIF
          </button>

          <button
            type='button'
            title='Emoji (coming soon)'
            aria-label='Emoji'
            className='flex h-8 w-8 items-center justify-center rounded text-gray-400 transition-colors hover:bg-white/10 hover:text-gray-200'
          >
            <svg
              className='h-5 w-5'
              viewBox='0 0 24 24'
              fill='none'
              stroke='currentColor'
              strokeWidth={2}
              strokeLinecap='round'
              strokeLinejoin='round'
            >
              <circle cx='12' cy='12' r='10' />
              <path d='M8 13s1.5 2 4 2 4-2 4-2' />
              <line x1='9' y1='9' x2='9.01' y2='9' />
              <line x1='15' y1='9' x2='15.01' y2='9' />
            </svg>
          </button>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

Output:

```text
# Lessons Learned

Shared knowledge base for the Harmony team. Add an entry whenever a mistake is caught, a better approach is discovered, or an AI agent is corrected.

---

## Template

**Date:** YYYY-MM-DD  
**Caught by:** [Human: @username] or [AI Agent: Copilot/Cursor]  
**Related Issue:** #<number> (optional)  
**Mistake / Situation:** One sentence describing what went wrong or what was unclear.  
**Rule / Fix:** The actionable rule derived — written so it prevents the same mistake next time.

---

## Log

<!-- Most recent entries at the top -->

**Date:** 2026-03-31  
**Caught by:** [AI Agent: Claude]  
**Related Issue:** N/A  
**Mistake / Situation:** E2E preflight initially verified seeded content but skipped critical auth assumptions, and the shared E2E helper encoded environment defaults too implicitly.  
**Rule / Fix:** E2E preconditions must verify every special-case fixture the suite depends on, including auth overrides, and shared test helpers should avoid hidden runtime defaults like hardcoded `NODE_ENV`.

**Date:** 2026-03-31  
**Caught by:** [AI Agent: Claude]  
**Related Issue:** N/A  
**Mistake / Situation:** The first true-E2E pass duplicated stack constants across TS and Node launcher files and allowed local server reuse, which made reruns stateful and brittle.  
**Rule / Fix:** For stateful E2E stacks, keep shared ports/URLs/test credentials in one cross-runtime JS module and force the backend launcher to restart/reset locally unless there is an explicit reason to preserve state.
```

```text
     1	import { expect, test, type Page, type TestInfo } from '@playwright/test';
     2	import {
     3	  DEV_ADMIN_EMAIL,
     4	  DEV_ADMIN_PASSWORD,
     5	  DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG,
     6	  SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL,
     7	  SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL,
     8	  SIGNUP_USER,
     9	} from './support/stack.shared.mjs';
    10	
    11	async function signUpMember(page: Page, testInfo: TestInfo) {
    12	  const { project, retry } = testInfo;
    13	  const normalizedProject = project.name.replace(/[^a-z0-9_-]/gi, '-').toLowerCase();
    14	  const normalizedRetry = `r${retry}`;
    15	  const emailLocalPart = `${SIGNUP_USER.username}-${normalizedProject}-${normalizedRetry}`.replace(
    16	    /_/g,
    17	    '-',
    18	  );
    19	  const email = `${emailLocalPart}@harmony.test`;
    20	  const username = `${SIGNUP_USER.username}_${normalizedProject}_${normalizedRetry}`.slice(0, 32);
    21	
    22	  await page.goto('/auth/signup');
    23	  await page.getByLabel(/^Email/).fill(email);
    24	  await page.getByLabel(/^Username/).fill(username);
    25	  await page.getByLabel('Display Name').fill(SIGNUP_USER.displayName);
    26	  await page.getByLabel(/^Password/).fill(SIGNUP_USER.password);
    27	  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Continue' }).click();
    28	}
    29	
    30	test.describe('True E2E auth and access flows', () => {
    31	  test('guest can read a seeded public channel', async ({ page }) => {
    32	    await page.goto(`/c/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`);
    33	
    34	    await expect(
    35	      page.getByRole('banner').getByText(SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.serverName, { exact: true }),
    36	    ).toBeVisible();
    37	    await expect(
    38	      page.getByRole('heading', { name: SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelName, exact: true }),
    39	    ).toBeVisible();
    40	    await expect(page.getByText(SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.welcomeText).first()).toBeVisible();
    41	    await expect(page.getByLabel('Join server promotion')).toBeVisible();
    42	  });
    43	
    44	  test('guest is denied access to a private channel', async ({ page }) => {
    45	    await page.goto(
    46	      `/c/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`,
    47	    );
    48	
    49	    await expect(page.getByText('This channel is private')).toBeVisible();
    50	    await expect(page.getByRole('link', { name: 'Create Account' })).toBeVisible();
    51	    await expect(page.getByRole('link', { name: 'Log In' })).toHaveAttribute(
    52	      'href',
    53	      new RegExp(
    54	        `returnUrl=%2Fc%2F${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}%2F${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}$`,
    55	      ),
    56	    );
    57	  });
    58	
    59	  test('dev admin can log in through the real backend and access a private channel', async ({
    60	    page,
    61	  }) => {
    62	    await page.goto(
    63	      `/channels/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}`,
    64	    );
    65	
    66	    await expect(page).toHaveURL(
    67	      new RegExp(
    68	        `/auth/login\\?returnUrl=%2Fchannels%2F${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}%2F${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}$`,
    69	      ),
    70	    );
    71	
    72	    await page.getByLabel('Email').fill(DEV_ADMIN_EMAIL);
    73	    await page.getByLabel('Password').fill(DEV_ADMIN_PASSWORD);
    74	    await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Log In' }).click();
    75	
    76	    await expect(page).toHaveURL(
    77	      new RegExp(
    78	        `/channels/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.serverSlug}/${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelSlug}$`,
    79	      ),
    80	    );
    81	    await expect(page.getByLabel(`Message #${SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL.channelName}`)).toBeVisible();
    82	  });
    83	
    84	  test('newly registered users join the seeded default server and land on the authenticated channel view', async ({
    85	    page,
    86	  }, testInfo) => {
    87	    await signUpMember(page, testInfo);
    88	
    89	    await expect(page).toHaveURL(
    90	      new RegExp(`/channels/${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}/${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelSlug}$`),
    91	    );
    92	    await expect(page.getByLabel(`Message #${SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.channelName}`)).toBeVisible();
    93	    await expect(page.getByText(SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL.welcomeText).first()).toBeVisible();
    94	  });
    95	});
```

```text
     1	import fs from 'node:fs';
     2	import path from 'node:path';
     3	
     4	export const FRONTEND_PORT = 3100;
     5	export const BACKEND_PORT = 4100;
     6	export const POSTGRES_PORT = 55432;
     7	export const REDIS_PORT = 56379;
     8	
     9	export const FRONTEND_URL = `http://localhost:${FRONTEND_PORT}`;
    10	export const BACKEND_URL = `http://localhost:${BACKEND_PORT}`;
    11	export const DATABASE_URL = `postgresql://harmony:harmony@localhost:${POSTGRES_PORT}/harmony_e2e`;
    12	export const REDIS_URL = `redis://:e2esecret@localhost:${REDIS_PORT}`;
    13	
    14	export const DEV_ADMIN_EMAIL = 'admin@harmony.dev';
    15	export const DEV_ADMIN_PASSWORD = 'admin';
    16	
    17	export const SIGNUP_USER = {
    18	  username: 'e2e_member',
    19	  displayName: 'E2E Member',
    20	  password: 'password123',
    21	};
    22	
    23	export function cleanEnv(env = process.env) {
    24	  return Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(env).filter(([, value]) => typeof value === 'string'));
    25	}
    26	
    27	export function frontendEnv(env = process.env) {
    28	  return {
    29	    ...cleanEnv(env),
    30	    PORT: String(FRONTEND_PORT),
    31	    NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL: BACKEND_URL,
    32	  };
    33	}
    34	
    35	export function backendEnv(env = process.env) {
    36	  return {
    37	    ...cleanEnv(env),
    38	    PORT: String(BACKEND_PORT),
    39	    DATABASE_URL,
    40	    REDIS_URL,
    41	    FRONTEND_URL,
    42	    JWT_ACCESS_SECRET: 'harmony-e2e-access-secret',
    43	    JWT_REFRESH_SECRET: 'harmony-e2e-refresh-secret',
    44	    JWT_ACCESS_EXPIRES_IN: '15m',
    45	    JWT_REFRESH_EXPIRES_DAYS: '7',
    46	    TWILIO_MOCK: 'true',
    47	    LOCAL_UPLOAD_BASE_URL: BACKEND_URL,
    48	  };
    49	}
    50	
    51	export function resolveE2EPaths(cwd = process.cwd()) {
    52	  const repoRoot = path.resolve(cwd, '..');
    53	  const backendDir = path.join(repoRoot, 'harmony-backend');
    54	
    55	  return {
    56	    repoRoot,
    57	    backendDir,
    58	    composeFile: path.join(backendDir, 'docker-compose.e2e.yml'),
    59	    composeProject: 'harmony-e2e',
    60	  };
    61	}
    62	
    63	function loadSeedSnapshot() {
    64	  const { backendDir } = resolveE2EPaths();
    65	  const seedPath = path.join(backendDir, 'src/dev/mock-seed-data.json');
    66	  try {
    67	    return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(seedPath, 'utf8'));
    68	  } catch (error) {
    69	    if (error && typeof error === 'object' && 'code' in error && error.code === 'ENOENT') {
    70	      throw new Error(
    71	        `Missing E2E seed snapshot at ${seedPath}. Ensure the Harmony backend mock seed files are present before running Playwright.`,
    72	      );
    73	    }
    74	
    75	    throw error;
    76	  }
    77	}
    78	
    79	const snapshot = loadSeedSnapshot();
    80	
    81	// Registration auto-joins this server in backend auth.service; keep this value
    82	// aligned with that backend contract and fail fast in launcher preflight if it drifts.
    83	export const DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG = 'harmony-hq';
    84	
    85	const defaultJoinServer = snapshot.servers.find(server => server.slug === DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG);
    86	if (!defaultJoinServer) {
    87	  throw new Error(
    88	    `Seed snapshot is missing the default join server slug "${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}".`,
    89	  );
    90	}
    91	
    92	const publicChannel = snapshot.channels.find(
    93	  channel => channel.serverId === defaultJoinServer.id && channel.visibility === 'PUBLIC_INDEXABLE',
    94	);
    95	if (!publicChannel) {
    96	  throw new Error(
    97	    `Seed snapshot is missing a public channel for server "${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}".`,
    98	  );
    99	}
   100	
   101	const privateChannel = snapshot.channels.find(
   102	  channel => channel.serverId === defaultJoinServer.id && channel.visibility === 'PRIVATE',
   103	);
   104	if (!privateChannel) {
   105	  throw new Error(
   106	    `Seed snapshot is missing a private channel for server "${DEFAULT_JOIN_SERVER_SLUG}".`,
   107	  );
   108	}
   109	
   110	const welcomeMessage = snapshot.messages.find(message => message.channelId === publicChannel.id);
   111	if (!welcomeMessage) {
   112	  throw new Error(`Seed snapshot is missing a message for public channel "${publicChannel.slug}".`);
   113	}
   114	
   115	export const SEEDED_PUBLIC_CHANNEL = {
   116	  serverSlug: defaultJoinServer.slug,
   117	  serverName: defaultJoinServer.name,
   118	  channelSlug: publicChannel.slug,
   119	  channelName: publicChannel.name,
   120	  welcomeText: welcomeMessage.content,
   121	};
   122	
   123	export const SEEDED_PRIVATE_CHANNEL = {
   124	  serverSlug: defaultJoinServer.slug,
   125	  channelName: privateChannel.name,
   126	  channelSlug: privateChannel.slug,
   127	};
```
